description:
What: After rebasing fz-dps-arc on origin/main (which adds +445 lines to ir_codegen.rs across PRs #12/#13/#14, primarily the pattern matrix arc), audit every new load/store site for the load/store-from-tagged-pointer pattern. The fz-o0u tag rewrite requires every heap deref to be preceded by untag_ptr (mask with PTR_UNTAG_MASK = !0b111) — any site main introduced before our rebase will SIGBUS on misaligned pointers.

Approach:
1. After rebase, diff src/ir_codegen.rs HEAD vs the pre-rebase tip on main: identify new b.ins().load and b.ins().store sites.
2. For each, determine whether the base value is a tagged FzValue pointer (TAG_PTR low bit set) or already-raw.
3. Output: either "no new tagged-base load sites" OR a list of file:line that need untag_ptr fixup.

Why: Without this audit, downstream tickets (fz-q9g.3 / C2 onward) risk SIGBUS in code regions we did not author. Investigate up front — no hidden surprises.

Removes: nothing (pure investigation).

Tests: re-run fixture matrix + full cargo test post-fixup; assert no SIGBUS regressions.

Note: this ticket is the natural sibling of fz-o0u — it extends the tag rewrite's discipline across newly-landed code.

comments.text: Audit conclusion (2026-05-18): premise invalidated by as-shipped fz-o0u. The ticket assumed TAG_PTR=0b001 requiring untag_ptr before heap deref; the actually-landed scheme uses TAG_PTR=0b000 (runtime/src/fz_value.rs:18), so tagged pointer values are raw pointers and loads work directly. Surveyed all load/store sites added to src/ir_codegen.rs since 004d1b0b (pre-q9g baseline) — only two new load sites (ir_codegen.rs:5591 Prim::TupleField and ir_codegen.rs:6532 tuple-arity TypeTest schema_id read); both load through TAG_PTR=0 pointers and are clean. No PTR_UNTAG_MASK exists in the tree because it is unnecessary. Closing as superseded — q9g.3 is no longer gated by this audit.

title: fz-dps.rebase: post-merge codegen audit for untag_ptr coverage
